instruction:
Assume that the continuous random variables $$x_{1}, x_{2}$$ are independent of each other, and the variances all exist $$x_{1}$$, the probability densities of $$x_{2}$$ are $$f_{1}( x), f_{2}(x)$$, the probability density of random variable $$Y_{1}$$ is $$f_{Y_{1}} ( y )=\frac{1} {2} ( f_ {1} ( y )+f_{2} ( y ) )$$, random variable $$\mathrm{Y}_{2}=\mathrm{~ ( x_{1}+x_{2} ) ~} / 2$$, then ().

Options:
A. EY1=EY2,DY1=DY2
B. EY1>EY2, DY1=DY2
C. EY1<ELY2, DY1=DY2
D. EY1>EY2, DY1<DY2
E. EY1<EY2, DY1<DY2
F. EY1=EY2,DY1<DY1<DY2
G. EY1>EY2, DY1>DY2
H. EY1<DY2, DY1=DY2
I. EY1=EY2, DY1<DY2
J. EY1=EY2, DY1>DY2
